 {"id":"bd-31dy","title":"glow conformance: real Go capture + strict output comparison","description":"Problem:
- `tests/conformance/go_reference/cmd/glow/main.go` writes reader fixtures with `output: "[needs Go capture]"` and `needs_runtime: true`.
- `tests/conformance/crates/glow/mod.rs` does not compare output to fixture output; it only checks non-empty output, so it can pass even when rendering is wrong.

Goal:
- Make glow conformance a real check against Go outputs (no placeholders; strict assertions).

Scope:
1) Update Go capture (`tests/conformance/go_reference/cmd/glow/main.go`) to actually render markdown using Go glamour and store the rendered output string in `expected_output.output` (remove `needs_runtime`).
2) Refresh `tests/conformance/fixtures/go_outputs/glow.json` using the capture toolchain.
3) Update Rust conformance (`tests/conformance/crates/glow/mod.rs`) to compare rendered output to the fixture output (with deterministic normalization for newlines/trailing whitespace; diffs on failure).

Acceptance:
- No `"[needs Go capture]"` or `needs_runtime` left in `tests/conformance/fixtures/go_outputs/glow.json`.
- Glow reader fixtures assert equality (after normalization).
- `cargo run -p charmed_conformance --bin run-conformance -- --crate glow --verbose` passes with 0 skipped.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RusticDeer","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:46:16.042358829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:11:08.859404796Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:11:08.859337581Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","glow"]}
 {"id":"bd-32ba","title":"SSH mode: run the showcase UI per SSH session","description":"For each SSH session, run the bubbletea program with the session stdin/stdout.

Implementation:
- use bubbletea custom I/O (`with_input`, `with_output`) so the runtime doesn’t touch local terminal state.
- ensure window size propagation from SSH PTY.

 {"id":"bd-32pp","title":"Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log","description":"Implement the *styled* log formatting layer for the Logs page using `charmed_log`.

Why:
- A flagship ops-style demo lives and dies by log readability.
- This showcases that `charmed_log` + lipgloss styling can produce beautiful, information-dense output.

Formatting requirements:
- Each log entry should include (where available):
  - timestamp (compact)
  - level (TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
  - service/component name
  - message
- Styling:
  - color-coded levels (warn/error pop, info muted)
  - consistent column alignment and truncation
  - preserve legibility in narrow terminals
- No-color mode:
  - degrade gracefully (no ANSI), still aligned and readable

Integration:
- Logs are produced by the demo domain simulation and by user actions.
- The formatted line should carry the structured fields needed for filtering (level/service/query) without re-parsing the rendered string.

Acceptance:
- Logs look great, are readable at a glance, and remain usable with thousands of lines.
- Deterministic formatting (important for snapshot/E2E artifacts).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:37.574456901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:16.801798652Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:16.801717932Z","close_reason":"Implemented LogFormatter with styled, aligned output supporting color-coded levels, no-color mode, and configurable column widths. Added 8 tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-33fe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-33ef","title":"Deep audit wish session manager lifecycle and reliability","description":"Random deep-audit pass: trace session manager flows, identify root-cause bugs, fix with tests and full gates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T23:48:30.959259356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:51:50.822886265Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T23:51:50.822819411Z","close_reason":"Implemented typed MaxSessionsReached error path for SessionManager, aligned docs/tests, and validated via wish+workspace gates","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-33fe","title":"demo_showcase data: structured log stream + formatting","description":"Create the underlying structured log stream used by the demo (domain simulation + user actions).

Why:
- The Logs page should not be "just strings". We want structured fields so filtering/search is fast and correct.
- It proves the integration story: domain layer emits events, UI renders + filters them.

Deliverables:
- A `LogEntry` type (or similar) with fields like:
  - timestamp / monotonic tick
  - level
  - service/component
  - message
  - optional correlation ids (job id, deployment id)
- A log buffer/stream:
  - append-only collection with a bounded retention policy (optional)
  - API to push new entries from simulation/actions

Integration:
- The Logs viewer uses this stream and a formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`) to render.
- Filtering operates on structured fields, not re-parsed rendered text.

Acceptance:
- Large log volumes remain responsive.
- Entries support correlation (wizard → job → logs) and filtering by level/service.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:53.990998123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336843390Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336748333Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured log stream with filtering. Enhanced LogEntry with tick, job_id, deployment_id correlation. Created LogStream with filtering by level/target/job/deployment/text/tick-range, count_by_level(), and bounded retention. Added 16 new tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33mv","title":"demo_showcase UX: define product concept + information architecture","description":"Pick a single cohesive "product" story and define the information architecture.

Recommendation (unless a better idea emerges): **Charmed Control Center** — an ops-style console for a fictional platform (services, deployments, jobs, logs, docs, settings).

Define:
- Primary navigation items (pages) and what each proves about the stack.
- Layout primitives (app chrome): header (status), sidebar (nav), main content, footer (hints).
- Core user journeys:
  - Navigate dashboards, drill into a service/job, view logs, open docs, run a wizard, browse files.

Acceptance:
- Clear page list with 1-2 sentences each describing: goal, components used, and what makes it "real".","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:04.263653172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:15:32.678896124Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:15:32.678828398Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive product concept doc at docs/demo_showcase/PRODUCT_CONCEPT.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33mv","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3449","title":"E2E scenario: wizard -> job -> logs correlation","description":"Scenario: complete the wizard to start a deployment/job, then verify it shows up elsewhere.

Steps:
- Open Wizard
- Fill inputs (including paste for one field)
- Confirm
- Observe job created in Jobs page
- Observe correlated log entries in Logs page

Assertions:
- Domain state changes are consistent across pages.
- Logs include fields referencing the created job/deployment.

Acceptance:
- Proves cross-page integration and the domain action API.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.600509120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:41:35.850246365Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:41:35.850177547Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-1x3q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-2qq7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3449","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
